subject: [SDC] Update pod limits and timeouts
commit_message:
[SDC] Update pod limits and timeouts

Add missing common flavor limits (and revisit some values)
Add fixed limits for single job containers and init containers (no need to have these configurable I think)
Reworked timeouts

Issue-ID: SDC-3282
Signed-off-by: ChrisC <christophe.closset@intl.att.com>
Change-Id: I2eb7122eed370978876ac496a68eebb51421595a

Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

(6 comments)

Hi Christophe,
thanks for the patch!

one question:
* are the "-t" necessary (I've created a template for readiness check without this option ;) )

and  I'd like you to keep the nindent 12 part (or 10, or 8 depending ;) ) as I'd like to remove spaces in common.resources in a short term

Patch Set 3:

> Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1
> 
> (6 comments)
> 
> Hi Christophe,
> thanks for the patch!
> 
> one question:
> * are the "-t" necessary (I've created a template for readiness check without this option ;) )
> 
> and  I'd like you to keep the nindent 12 part (or 10, or 8 depending ;) ) as I'd like to remove spaces in common.resources in a short term

Hi Sylvain,
Thanks for the review!

the -t is not strictly necessary but my intention was to avoid seeing Failed pods in SDC deployments (maybe it's just me, but I don't like seeing failed things in my beautiful SDC startup :), because it is very sequential (and takes quite some time): SDC starts first a cassandra provisionning pod (CS-init) which takes about 8min to complete (not counting the fact that this waits for Cassandra cluster to be up), then the onboard Backend starts (takes a few mins) then another provisionning starts through the back end (another few  mins), then the main back end start ... so all this adds up and typically you end up seeing lots of failed jobs because they timeout after 10mins. 
So I don't know what is best : either keep the readiness waiting for a while or leave them to fail and wait another instance to succeed, what is your preference ?

For the indent, I tried keeping the nindent but then I had problems with limits not recognized because the template adds its additional hardcoded indent which makes them wrongly formatted, I'll try again to be sure and if it works, i can update them.

Christophe
